verification_gate: Red tests fail first for: Mount table specified; Overlay vs bind decisions recorded; Matches tier map T1.2; green only when all pass with a recorded proof record.

gate_result.red_first: gates executed red with the design artifact absent (2/2 files failing), green at HEAD (3/3)
gate_result.table_evidence: planning-spine-v0/docs/envelope_mount_design.json specifies the complete nine-row mount table: private tmpfs /, ro-bind /nix, minimal ro /etc (resolv.conf/passwd/group only), fresh procfs, minimal devtmpfs, per-envelope tmpfs /tmp, tmpfs home overlay, explicit rw durable binds, and on-demand GPU/device dev-binds — each with mechanism, writability, tier, and a substantive rationale.
gate_result.decision_evidence: Four named decisions with rationale: tmpfs-not-overlay for the private root (declared-only presentation; unprivileged overlayfs blocked by the same AppArmor policy), overlay-as-tmpfs-not-overlayfs for home (residuals invisible, no write-back channel), bind-not-copy for the store, and explicit-rw-bind-per-durable-root for durable state.
gate_result.tier_map_evidence: Every mount row's tier is a T1.2 tier-map tier; the durable row names the map's durable roots (meta/var/lib/postgresql); the spec also proves the design is IMPLEMENTED — the shipped engine constructs exactly these primitives (--tmpfs /, --ro-bind /nix, --tmpfs $HOME).
gate_result.product_execution_evidence: The design is validated against the live engine source that ARCHBP-065..071 proved by execution.

proof_summary: ARCHBP-129 is complete: the envelope mount design is fully specified — private tmpfs root, read-only /nix, tmpfs home overlay, minimal /etc, and explicit durable-state binds — with every overlay-vs-bind decision recorded and justified, matched to the T1.2 tier map, and proven implemented by the shipped yzx-envelope engine.
